fieldDescription: Should the worker pool condition of NPC markets be visible
The worker pool grows with the population growth progress.

fieldName: The Default Ratio of Surplus Value
fieldDescription: Defines the split between worker wages and surplus output. Higher values mean lower wages. Used by NPC markets.

fieldName: The Maximum Ratio of Surplus Value
fieldDescription: The maximum value for player owned colonies.

fieldName: The Monthly Value of Labor Power
fieldDescription: The value in credits produced by a single worker per month.
Used by industries without a specified RoVC entry.
Used by heavy industry, refining and faction ship production.
Used by mining and fuel production.
Used by space related industries.
Higher values encourage more demand fulfillment.
Multiplier for the cost of workers assigned to meet local demand. A value of 1 stops prioritizing production for local demand. Heavy on performance when allocation passes are high.
Multiplier for the cost of workers assigned to meet faction demand. A value of 1 disables it. Should ideally be higher than Local Worker Cost Mult.
Allocation is prioritized to meet local demand by a factor of 1 + x.
Allocation is prioritized to meet faction demand by a factor of 1 + x.
Workers are allocated to meet demand by a factor of 1 + x.
Number of times the solver re-evaluates deficits and adjusts worker assignments. Computation time grows linearly with the value. Gains become minimal after 6 passes.
Workers of NPC markets will be re-allocated every x days.
Multiplier applied to trade mission targets when allocating hulls. Provides a safety margin to ensure demand is fully met.

fieldName: Ship Allocation Market Weight Per Size
fieldDescription: Base weight per market size factor when prioritizing trade mission assignments.
Exponent applied to market size to calculate the size factor for automatic ship orders.
Bonus multiplier to target combat power based on faction aggression doctrine. Higher aggression increases combat oriented hull production.
Multiplier applied to combat power targets when ordering ships.
Combat power target floor used by factions when ordering ships.
Runs the economy loop inside a separate thread.
Re-allocates the workers after loading a save. Includes quick-saves as well.
The starting credits each colony has. Used for player colonies as well.
Sets the maximum amount of credits you can withdraw from your colonies per month.
The maximum share of the profit made by colonies that can automatically be transferred to the player.
Markets will trade goods every x days.
Days of demand markets aim to stockpile, affecting imports, exports, and base prices.
Limits how many days of demand can be satisfied per trade run to reduce volatility. Values lower than Trade Interval will cause constant deficits.
Multiplier for excess stock required before a market begins exporting.
Adjusts prices for goods traded between markets of the same faction. Set to 0 for free in-faction trade.
The relationship hit for imposing an embargo. 1 means 100 relation points.
Minimum relationship between factions for trade to occur. Factions below this threshold will not trade.

fieldName: Max Displayed Ships in Trade Mission
fieldDescription: Maximum number of allocated ship types shown in a trade mission widget.
Ceiling for minimum trade amount filter slider (in tons).
Fraction of sector-wide excess and demand that the informal sector can move every trade interval. Lower values limit informal trade volume.
Multiplier to maintenance supply cost for idle ships inside the faction inventory. A value of 1 disables the discount.
Monthly wages per crew member operating the faction ships inventory.
Wage multiplier for idle crew operating the faction ships inventory.
The speed in light-years at which trade fleets travel in hyperspace. Used by the simulation.
Cost multiplier for fuel purchases made to initiate trade when no fuel is stockpiled.
Base fee in credits per trade mission when using independent haulers.
Fee per ton of cargo (including fuel and crew) transported.
Percentage of the total cargo value taken as fee.
Base hazard pay in credits added for dangerous routes.
Multiplier for hazard pay based on the required combat power of the mission.
Maximum number of planned orders displayed in the Planned Orders and Assembly Line panels. Additional orders are hidden for performance.
Number of assembly lines available to NPC factions.
Starting number of assembly lines available to the player faction.
Ratio of the hull's value returned as scrap metals when discarded.
The maximum fraction of a colony's stockpile that can be looted in a single raid.
Base multiplier for loot quantity from raids (applies before other modifiers).
